description: Designer-coordinated counterpart-pair mirror. Kanto Gen I's most explicitly-symmetrical version-exclusive pair: Pokedex-adjacent (125/126), Blue-vs-Red exclusives, mirrored industrial habitats (Power Plant / Pokemon Mansion), parallel trade-holding-izer evolution mechanics added in the same generation (Gen IV: Electirizer / Magmarizer), and step-for-step mirrored three-stage lines (Elekid-Electabuzz-Electivire / Magby-Magmar-Magmortar). Both printed in the same set (Rebel Clash). The canonical evidence was already inline in Electabuzz's wave-30 trivia bullet 'Designer counterpart pair' — Edgelord is honoring that bullet's implicit cross-reference by formalizing it as a graph edge. Edgelord notes Magmar still lacks a Trivia section, which is a janitor-tier flag, but it does NOT block this edge — the pair-canon is established on Electabuzz's side with Bulbapedia citation and is symmetric by definition.
citations: [Bulbapedia: Magmar (Pokemon), Electabuzz (Pokemon), List of Pokemon with counterparts]
other_candidates_considered: Chewtle cross-print mirror (FUS-080 + VIV-038) — clean standard 2-print mirror, but mundane next to a designer-confirmed counterpart-pair canon. Worth a follow-up Edgelord pass when a slot is otherwise unspoken for.

Future kanto-counterpart-pairs hub or character node — the franchise has multiple designer-coordinated Gen I counterpart pairs (Magmar/Electabuzz; Plusle/Minun (Gen III, not Kanto); the Tauros/Miltank single-gender pair if you stretch the category). If 2+ such pairs land in corpus, Nodeley should consider a designer-coordinated-pokemon-pairs cohort node analogous to elemental-monkey-trio. Currently 1/N pairs in corpus, premature.
Magmar Trivia section gap — bbl-triviabot follow-up needed. Janitor flag, not Edgelord work.
